title: I built an AI-powered crypto trading signals app — looking for feedback
author: Historical_Horror_16
selftext:
Hey everyone 👋

I’ve been working on a project called CryptoXHunter, an app that uses AI models to generate LONG and SHORT crypto trading signals across major cryptocurrencies.

The goal wasn’t to create another “get rich quick” tool, but rather something that helps traders analyze trends and market movements more efficiently.

Current features: • AI-generated trading signals

• LONG & SHORT opportunities

• Real-time market analysis

• Coverage of 8 major cryptocurrencies

• Simple and clean interface

I’m currently looking for honest feedback from traders and crypto users:

What features would you actually want in an app like this?

What do most signal apps do wrong?

Would alerts, sentiment analysis, or portfolio tracking be useful additions?

[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cryptoadviserapp](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cryptoadviserapp)

Happy to answer questions and improve the product based on feedback 🚀

comments.author: carlo60trav
comments.body: I use several signal providers and integrate their bots with the Altrady platform, which allows me to leverage many other professional skills and tools. Signal bots alone are not enough to be a trader.

comments.author: Fun-Society-1763
comments.body: This looks super clean! Generating signals is definitely the hard part, but validating them is where most people get stuck. Have you run these signals through a robust backtester across different market regimes? If you need a sandbox to test your AI's historical performance, you might want to check out QuantPlace. You can plug your signal logic into their backtesting engine with alternative data to see how it handles drawdowns before users put real money on it

replies.author: Historical_Horror_16
replies.body:
Yes — ive been validating with walk-forward testing to avoid overfitting.

The model was trained on 2017–2021 data, then tested on unseen 2022 market conditions. After that, retrained up to 2022 and tested on 2023, and so on.

The goal is to simulate real-world deployment as closely as possible instead of testing on data already seen during training. Still improving the robustness across different market regimes and volatility periods

replies.author: Historical_Horror_16
replies.body:
Also to clarify — the goal isn’t to build an auto-trading bot.
It’s more of an AI trading assistant that helps users with market analysis, probabilities, and trade ideas while keeping the final decision in the trader’s hands.

I want it to be a tool assistant for smarter decision-making, not a “guaranteed signals” app.
